title: Opening Narration
readAloud:
Welcome to The Bean Rounders.

The Sea's Fury lurches violently beneath your feet as another wave crashes over the deck.

Rain lashes sideways through the darkness.

Above you, sails snap like thunder.

Somewhere in the rigging, timber splinters.

For a brief moment lightning tears across the sky, turning the ocean white.

The wave towering over the port rail looks almost as high as the mast.

For nearly two months, this ship has been your home.

The three of you joined the Sea's Fury in Waterdeep, chasing rumours of an ancient idol hidden somewhere deep within the jungles of Chult. An idol so old that maps stopped marking its resting place over a hundred years ago.

The journey south had gone well enough.

You made port in Baldur's Gate.

Passed through Athkatla.

Took on supplies and cargo in Zazesspur.

Each stop carrying you further from civilisation and closer to fortune.

The plan was simple.

Reach Chult.

Find a guide.

Explore the jungle.

Get rich.

Then came Calimport.

Or rather...

Calimport never came.

A pirate blockade in the harbour forced Captain Thorne to sail on without taking on the provisions needed for the crossing.

Fresh food disappeared days ago.

Water is rationed by the cup.

The crew are exhausted.

The passengers are hungry.

Everyone aboard the Sea's Fury has spent the last week looking to the horizon and praying for land.

Captain Thorne swore Chult was close.

One more night at sea.

Port by morning.

Then the storm arrived.

The Sea's Fury groans beneath your feet once more.

Not the sound of a ship sailing.

The sound of a ship losing a fight.

Cargo crates break loose and slide across the deck.

Crew rush past carrying ropes and lanterns.

Someone screams from somewhere below.

Then Captain Thorne's voice cuts through the chaos.

"ALL HANDS ON DECK!"

"SECURE THOSE LINES!"

"MOVE, MOVE, MOVE!"

Another wave crashes over the rail.

The storm doesn't care who you are.

It doesn't care where you're going.

It certainly doesn't care about treasure.

Tonight there is only one question.

Will the Sea's Fury survive until dawn?

So tell me...

Where are each of you right now?

And what do you do?
